title: Symptom Log Entry
description: The canonical shape of a SymptomLogs record — docs/29-PHASE-2A-TECHNICAL-PLAN.md §4's already-locked sheet and §9's architecture, and the concrete storage of docs/33-DOMAIN-MODEL.md §3.2's Symptom Log. Backs the patient-facing Symptom Tracker dashboard card (quick-log form + most-recent-value summary) and full history page (docs/29 §5). See symptom-log.md for the create-only lifecycle, the all-four-mandatory field decision, and the condition_slug taxonomy note.

record_id.description: The permanent identity of this entry. A UUID, generated once at creation, never reused or changed, SQL-migration-safe by design (ADR-006). Not currently used to fetch a single entry (docs/41 §12: no get_by_id exists in this schema version) — reserved for a future per-entry detail view if one is ever approved.

patient_id.description: The Patient Identity this entry belongs to (docs/33 §1.2, ADR-002). Always session-derived at write time and at read time — never accepted from the request body.

logged_at.description: Server-set at write time (foundationNowIso_()), never patient-editable in this schema version (docs/41 §10 Q2) — unlike ConsultationHistory's entry_date, there is no separate patient-chosen 'this happened on a different day' field. Also this entry's sole sort key (descending) for the full history list.

severity.description: 1-10 scale, patient self-reported. Mandatory on every entry (docs/41 §10 Q1) — validated server-side regardless of client-side input constraints.

sleep.description: 1-10 scale, patient self-reported. Mandatory on every entry (docs/41 §10 Q1).

energy.description: 1-10 scale, patient self-reported. Mandatory on every entry (docs/41 §10 Q1).

stress.description: 1-10 scale, patient self-reported. Mandatory on every entry (docs/41 §10 Q1).

notes.description: Optional free-text note. Empty string when not provided — the same legitimately-empty sentinel convention consultation-history.schema.json's source_ref already established, reused here rather than inventing a new null-handling rule. Escaped for display wherever rendered; never trusted as markup.

condition_slug.description: Optional tag reusing the canonical condition taxonomy (shared/constants/condition-slugs.json, docs/20 §5). Empty string when not provided (same sentinel convention as notes/source_ref). Deliberately typed as a plain string, not an enum, so this schema does not itself hardcode the slug list — apps-script/FoundationSymptomLog.gs validates a non-empty value against shared/constants/condition-slugs.json's list at write time.
